human_review_reason: Minor issues are procedural (thin content, vague forward-looking language) rather than factual errors. The record is largely accurate and well-structured. Human review not required; automated routing to review_queue is appropriate given the borderline confidence score.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: Consider adding that this is an opening statement for a parliamentary inquiry, which means it sets the RBA's initial position but does not constitute final policy. The mid-2026 consultations will be the substantive opportunity for stakeholder input.
corrected_fields.market_structure_context: The testimony discusses structural reforms to Australia's payment system including: (1) expanded regulatory remit under amended PSRA covering new payment participants and systems; (2) upcoming consultations on three-party card schemes, mobile wallets, BNPL and e-commerce platforms; (3) account-to-account payments modernization workstream; (4) Project Acacia exploring tokenised asset market infrastructure. The source outlines the regulatory framework but does not quantify balance sheet implications or specify implementation timelines beyond mid-2026 consultations. As an opening statement, this testimony establishes the RBA's preliminary position for the inquiry rather than announcing finalized policy.
